source_pr.title: gctuenr: fix unstable test TestFinalizer
source_pr.body:

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

```release-note

```

symptoms: TestFinalizer is unstable because Go finalizer execution/GC timing is nondeterministic; the finalizer callback can run after the test considers the finalizer object collected, triggering a fatal assertion.
failure_signature: TestFinalizer: "cannot exec finalizer callback after f has been gc" (util/gctuner/finalizer_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by timing-sensitive runtime behavior: Go finalizers and garbage collection run asynchronously and can be scheduled unpredictably in CI, so the finalizer callback could still run and trip the test's fatal check. The fix adjusts GC settings (SetGCPercent) and adds an atomic stop flag to prevent the callback from failing the test after teardown, making the test robust against finalizer timing.
fix_pattern: Make finalizer-based tests robust to GC timing by adjusting GC settings and guarding the callback with a stop/teardown flag.

evidence: evidence: gctuenr: fix unstable test TestFinalizer
